items.title: US military strikes Iranian boats and missile launch sites

children.content: US Centcom says the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

articles.title: Why Trump's push to expand his Abraham Accords was met with silence

children.content: As the US president pushes for a deal with Iran, he is reviving one of the signature foreign policy projects of his first term: the Abraham Accords. Here's what to know about the series of agreements, and whether Arab countries will answer his call to formally establish diplomatic ties with Israel.

image.altText: A photo of Benjamin Netanyahu, Donald Trump and Abdullah bin Zayed sitting at a long table, holding up copies of signed deals

articles.title: Trump calls on Arab nations to 'immediately sign the Abraham Accords'

children.content: Donald Trump has boldly tied negotiations to end his war with Iran to a swathe of Arab nations formally establishing diplomatic ties with Israel.

image.altText: Israeli PM Netanyahu shakes hands with US President Donald Trump, who points at him.

articles.title: Inside the Tehran press conference where the ABC was the only Western media present

children.content: While on assignment inside Iran, our reporter asked a foreign ministry official about the regime's plan for the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesperson responded that "there is no toll" but also signalled crossing the strait will not be entirely free.

image.altText: ABC reporter Karishma Vyas sits among other reporters and asks a question

items.title: Travel company collapses, leaving customers stranded

children.content: An Australian travel company selling heavily discounted international holidays has collapsed, leaving customers scrambling and their plans in disarray.

image.altText: A billboard that reads 'Travel more, spend less: AVG travels'

items.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death

children.content: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has been concluded.

image.altText: A group of Aboriginal mourners walking through a coles supermarket, while holding branches.

items.title: Scathing warning to people harassing royal commission witnesses

children.content: Four officers were present at the event when the shooting started, with Jewish security organisation Community Security Group NSW denied its request to have police on patrol for the entirety of the event.

items.title: Angus Taylor pressures PM to detail small business CGT carve-outs in QT

children.content: The opposition leader has pressured the prime minister to reveal details of small business carve-outs for capital gains tax discount changes. Follow live.

image.altText: Angus Taylor looks at his backbench in the lower house.

items.title: Iron ore processing project dumped by BHP for not being profitable enough

children.content: Internal BHP documents reveal how the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara to cut carbon emissions before backing away from it entirely.

gallery.altText: A photo of an iron ore mine is ripped down the middle and replaced by a photo of a BHP building.

items.title: Santos boss 'can't believe' gas reservation would force loss-making prices

children.content: The federal government's draft framework would require gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their exports to the domestic market, with limited exceptions, which gas producers warn would "undermine" future supply.

image.altText: Kevin Gallagher wears a suit at a press conference in Darwin.

items.title: Police taskforce investigating Dezi Freeman arrests two men

children.content: Detectives investigating the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman have arrested two men in Victoria's north-east.

items.title: K-pop triumphs at American Music Awards as Pussycat Dolls lead noughties nostalgia

children.content: Taylor Swift led the night with eight nominations but didn't take home a single award, while 80s rock star Billy Idol was honoured with a Lifetime Achievement Award.

items.title: Hypersonic missile attack a sign of Putin's 'bruised ego', expert says

children.content: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv, using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war, and warns foreigners to leave the city ahead of further strikes.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' under exclusion order not returning to Australia, ABC confirms

children.content: The majority of the final group of Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp for the families of killed and captured Islamic State militants are expected to arrive in Australia soon. Here's what we know about them.

image.altText: A group of three women wearing burqas walk along a camp in Syria.

items.title: Fan in Australian jersey catches Aussie-hit homer in 15-year first

children.content: Curtis Mead hit two home runs for Washington against fellow Australian Travis Bazzana's Cleveland side as two Australian-born players faced off in an MLB game for the first time since 2017. But it was a man in an Australian supporters shirt who stole the show.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, one mum says MDMA therapy saved her life

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

image.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

items.title: One woman will make Japanese history by going on maternity leave

children.content: A Japanese mayor will take maternity leave this year, becoming the first in the country's history to do so while in office.

items.title: The many phases of Miles Davis, who would have turned 100 today

children.content: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.

items.title: 'Catastrophic failure' after drones malfunction at Vivid Sydney show

children.content: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7.30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.

items.title: Power price drops for most customers as batteries flatten peak

children.content: Surging levels of renewable energy and better reliability from coal-fired generators are set to give consumers a break, with benchmark power prices to fall up to 10 per cent for consumers and more for small businesses.

items.title: Australian stocks fall as US strikes dampen investor sentiment

items.title: Teary council officer tells ICAC they 'should be ashamed of yourselves'

items.title: Late pastoralist's family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for bushfire

items.title: Bringing Them Home inaction a 'woeful failure', ex-commissioner says

children.content: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.
